generated_oml:
// Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 thermistor inside the incubator (side A), accuracy ±0.5°C"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempDataTransmitted1
]
instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 thermistor inside the incubator (side B), accuracy ±0.5°C"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempDataTransmitted2
]
instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 thermistor measuring ambient temperature outside the incubator, accuracy ±0.5°C"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTempDataTransmitted
]

// Data Transmitted
instance InternalTempDataTransmitted1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]
instance InternalTempDataTransmitted2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]
instance ExternalTempDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// Data Instances
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑stamped temperature measurement record from the DS18S20 sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempDataTransmitted1
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempDataTransmitted2
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTempDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LowLevelDriverEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SciPyOptimizerEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBEnabler
]
instance ModelOutputDataB : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predicted internal temperature from ThermalModelB used by the Kalman filter and co‑simulation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ThermalModelB
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler
]

// Models
instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model assuming no heat absorption by the heatbed contains two free heat‑transfer parameters"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SciPyOptimizerEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler
]
instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Advanced analytical model that captures thermal inertia of the heatbed contains four free parameters"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SciPyOptimizerEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler
]

// Enablers
instance LowLevelDriverEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python script running on RaspberryPi that reads sensors and publishes data to RabbitMQ using a safe protocol"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]
instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker used to transmit sensor data between the physical twin and the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataReceptionService
]
instance InfluxDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB time‑series database that stores recorded temperature data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService
]
instance SciPyOptimizerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SciPy optimisation routines used for calibration of the thermal models"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]
instance KalmanFilterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter estimator that fuses sensor measurements and thermal model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]
instance CoSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Co‑simulation engine for running what‑if analyses with historical or live data"
    DTDFVocab:enables WhatIfSimulationService
]
instance DashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Frontend dashboard for real‑time visualization of sensor data and model estimates"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDashboardService
]

// Services
instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Streaming of raw sensor data to RabbitMQ for integration with the twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides RawTemperatureDataInsight
]
instance DataReceptionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Handling of incoming RabbitMQ messages within the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides RecordedTemperatureDataInsight
]
instance DataStorageService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Storage of recorded temperature data in InfluxDB"
    DTDFVocab:provides RecordedTemperatureDataInsight
]
instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "On‑demand calibration of ThermalModelB using least‑squares fitting"
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibratedModelInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides RecalibrateModelAction
]
instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detection of temperature anomalies via Kalman filter residuals"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatbedTemperatureEstimateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides AlertAction
]
instance WhatIfSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of what‑if simulations using co‑simulation of historical/live data"
    DTDFVocab:provides WhatIfSimulationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides RunWhatIfSimulationAction
]
instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Self‑adaptation loop that triggers new experiments and re‑optimises controller settings"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptationDecisionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ScheduleNewExperimentAction
    DTDFVocab:provides UpdateControllerSettingsAction
    DTDFVocab:provides RecalibrateModelAction
]
instance RealTimeDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Realtime dashboard rendering of measured, estimated and predicted temperatures"
    DTDFVocab:provides RawTemperatureDataInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatbedTemperatureEstimateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides WhatIfSimulationInsight
]
instance AlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Send anomaly alert to stakeholders"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Insights
instance RawTemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Raw temperature sensor data as received from the DS18S20 sensors"
]
instance RecordedTemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Temperature data stored in the time‑series database for analysis and visualization"
]
instance CalibratedModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Updated free‑parameter sets of ThermalModelB after calibration"
]
instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of temperature anomalies based on Kalman filter residual thresholds"
]
instance HeatbedTemperatureEstimateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimated unmeasured heatbed temperature derived from the Kalman filter"
]
instance WhatIfSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results of what‑if analysis from co‑simulation of thermal models"
]
instance AdaptationDecisionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Decision made by the self‑adaptation loop to re‑calibrate, re‑optimise or schedule experiments"
]

// Actions
instance RecalibrateModelAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic recalibration of ThermalModelB using newly recorded data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance UpdateActuatorSettingsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote update of actuator GPIO settings for heatbed and fan"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance RunWhatIfSimulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of a what‑if simulation with specified scenario parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance ScheduleNewExperimentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Scheduling of a new experimental run based on detected plant behavior changes"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance UpdateControllerSettingsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Reconfiguration of the bang‑bang controller setpoints and hysteresis limits"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric heatbed delivered via RaspberryPi GPIO output switches on/off to control temperature"
]
instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric fan permanently powered to distribute heat evenly inside the incubator"
]

// Controller
instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "Bang‑bang temperature controller on RaspberryPi with setpoint 38.5°C and tolerance ±0.5°C defines hysteresis band via LL and UL, with hold‑time parameter H and response parameter C"
]

// Driver
instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "Low‑level driver running on RaspberryPi that interfaces with DS18S20 sensors and actuators"
]

// Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LowLevelDriver
    base:contains BangBangController
    base:contains HeatbedActuator
    base:contains FanActuator
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:contains ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:contains RabbitMQEnabler
    base:contains InfluxDBEnabler
    base:contains DashboardEnabler
    base:contains LowLevelDriverEnabler
    base:contains SciPyOptimizerEnabler
    base:contains KalmanFilterEnabler
    base:contains CoSimulationEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands are sent from the DT to the physical incubator through the same RabbitMQ broker. The controller, running on the Raspberry Pi, receives command messages to turn the heatbed or fan on/off. Messages are sent in a structured format (e.g., JSON) and validated by a safe protocol implemented in the low‑level driver to ensure that actuators are only commanded when safe to do so. After executing a command, the controller publishes its control state back to RabbitMQ, providing feedback and confirmation that the actuation was successful. Conflict resolution is handled by the bang‑bang controller logic, enforcing a heating duration and a waiting period so that only one actuation command is active at a time."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The low‑level driver periodically polls sensor data and publishes it to the RabbitMQ communication server; the controller on the Raspberry Pi subscribes to these updates, executes control logic, and publishes actuator commands back to the broker. No numeric frequency or latency specifications are provided in the documentation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin is orchestrated through a set of inter‑linked components: a RabbitMQ communication server acts as the central message bus; a low‑level driver aggregates sensor data and publishes to RabbitMQ while consuming control commands; a bang‑bang controller consumes temperature data from RabbitMQ, enforces temperature limits via heating and fan actuators, and publishes control state; a calibration module (non‑linear least‑squares solver) is invoked on demand to refine the parameters of Model B; a Kalman‑filter module receives model predictions and sensor measurements from the time‑series database to perform anomaly detection and heat‑bed temperature estimation; a visualization component streams time‑series data to a dashboard; and a self‑adaptation loop schedules experiments, re‑configures the controller and filter, executes co‑simulation for what‑if studies, and iteratively optimizes controller parameters. This constellation implements the feedback loop described in the case study, integrating modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimisation within a single twin."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured by an on‑demand calibration routine (section 3.3) that employs a least‑squares fit to align simulated temperature trajectories with the recorded sensor data.  Model B, with four calibrated parameters, provides a better fit than Model A because it captures the heatbed’s thermal inertia.  Validation is performed by comparing simulation outputs to measured temperatures and by evaluating fit metrics such as mean‑squared error (exact figures not provided).  Sensor uncertainty—±0.5 °C—contributes to overall model uncertainty, which is reduced by periodic recalibration.  An anomaly detection mechanism based on a Kalman filter monitors deviations of the internal temperature from the model; when anomalies are detected, a self‑adaptation loop triggers a new calibration and re‑optimises the controller.  Continuous monitoring, real‑time data logging, and on‑demand calibration constitute the quality‑assurance strategy for both models and data."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The system uses RabbitMQ, a lightweight message broker, as the central communication hub. All components publish/subscribe to topic exchanges over the same local network. The Docker or process details are not specified, nor are bandwidth or latency thresholds. Reliability and security specifics are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All twin software runs locally on a single on‑premise Raspberry Pi (edge) device. The Pi hosts the RabbitMQ broker, the low‑level driver, the bang‑bang controller, and a lightweight time‑series database for real‑time dashboarding. No external cloud hosting is referenced; the deployment model is a singular edge installation with the Pi acting both as physical twin controller and digital twin host. Availability and scalability are implied by the Raspberry Pi’s limited capacity, but the documents do not specify redundancy or scaling strategies."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is realised through the RabbitMQ message broker, which enables decoupled, topic‑based publishing and subscribing between the low‑level driver, controller, and higher‑level analytics services. The broker is open‑source and can be deployed on‑premises or in the cloud, allowing plug‑in of additional enterprise applications or other digital twins if needed. No other external protocols or data‑exchange standards are explicitly referenced in the documents."
]
